DECLARATION OF CINDY HORTON IN SUPPORT OF OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENA FOR BUSINESS RECORDS TO NAPA POLICE DEPARTMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 100109698) FILED BY DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY, A CORPORATION BOCK, MICHAEL EDWARD , AN INDIVIDUAL

DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE SEP-8-2020 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF BRUCE TREGASKIS' MOTION TO QUASH DEPOSITION SUBPOENA FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS FROM NAPA POLICE DEPARTMENT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO QUASH THE PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY SUBOENA FOR THE PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS FROM NAPA POLICE IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART AS FOLLOWS: (1) THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO THOSE DOCUMENTS CALLED FOR BY ATTACHMENT 3, PARAGRAPH 2: ALL DOCUMENTS PERTAINING TO ANY ARREST OF, OR TRAFFIC COLLISION REPORT INVOLVING BRUCE TREGASKIS. THESE RECORDS ON THEIR FACE ARE CLEARLY PRIVATE AND DEFENDANT HAS MADE NO SHOWING TO OVERCOME THE PRIVACY CONCERNS; (2) THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO THOSE DOCUMENTS CALLED FOR BY ATTACHMENT 3, PARAGRAPH 1: ALL DOCUMENTS INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO POLICE REPORTS, PHOTOS, AND RECORDINGS OF INTERVIEWS INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO BADYCAM FOOTAGE AND RAW DATA FILES (PRODUCED ON USB STICK IS FINE) RELATED TO TRAFFIC COLLISION REPORT NO. NPD19-006888. ALTHOUGH SOME OF THESE RECORDS MAY BE PRIVATE, PLAINTIFF BY FILING HIS LAWSUIT HAS WAIVED ANY CLAIM OF PRIVACY; (3) THE COURT WILL ASSESS NO MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST EITHER PARTY. COURT TO SIGN THE PROPOSED ORDER SUBMITTED BY EMAIL BY PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL. JUDGE PRO TEM: PAUL RENNE; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Documents From Napa Police Department
Off Calendar Sep-08-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDECONFERENCE):
Atty. DANIEL MILLER, Esq. of Wilshire Law Firm (Tel: 213-381-9988) for Plaintiff Bruce Tregaskis;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF BRUCE TREGASKIS' Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Documents From Napa Police Department.

Plaintiff's counsel stipulates to the matter being decided by the judge pro tem. Both counsel and the judge pro tem appearing remotely authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO QUASH THE PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY SUBOENA FOR THE PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS FROM NAPA POLICE IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART AS FOLLOWS:
(1) THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO THOSE DOCUMENTS CALLED FOR BY ATTACHMENT 3, PARAGRAPH 2: ALL DOCUMENTS PERTAINING TO ANY ARREST OF, OR TRAFFIC COLLISION REPORT INVOLVING BRUCE TREGASKIS. THESE RECORDS ON THEIR FACE ARE CLEARLY PRIVATE AND DEFENDANT HAS MADE NO SHOWING TO OVERCOME THE PRIVACY CONCERNS.
(2) THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO THOSE DOCUMENTS CALLED FOR BY ATTACHMENT 3, PARAGRAPH 1: ALL DOCUMENTS INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO POLICE REPORTS, PHOTOS, AND RECORDINGS OF INTERVIEWS INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO BADYCAM FOOTAGE AND RAW DATA FILES (PRODUCED ON USB STICK IS FINE) RELATED TO TRAFFIC COLLISION REPORT NO. NPD19-006888. ALTHOUGH SOME OF THESE RECORDS MAY BE PRIVATE, PLAINTIFF BY FILING HIS LAWSUIT HAS WAIVED ANY CLAIM OF PRIVACY.
(3) THE COURT WILL ASSESS NO MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST EITHER PARTY

Court to sign the proposed order submitted by email by plaintiff's counsel.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
